data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Tapestry (TPR) After A 115% One-Year Share Price Surge?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Tapestry's share price still offers value after a strong run, it helps to step back and look at what the current price might be implying.
  • The stock most recently closed at US$145.89, with returns of 13.0% year to date and 114.9% over the last year. Over the last 7 days the share price declined 6.4%, and over the last 30 days it was roughly flat with a 0.2% slip.
  • Recent headlines have focused on Tapestry's position in the luxury space and how investor sentiment has reacted to broader sector moves. This helps explain some of the share price swings you may have seen. Coverage has often centered on how brands in this group are being reassessed by the market in terms of future growth, competitive strength and resilience.
  • Tapestry currently has a valuation score of 0 out of 6. The next step is to look at how different valuation methods assess the stock and then circle back to an even more complete way of thinking about value later in the article.

Tapestry scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Tapestry Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value using a required rate of return.

For Tapestry, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections rather than earnings multiples. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $1.65b. Analyst estimates and further extrapolated figures indicate projected Free Cash Flow of $2.11b in 2035, with intermediate years such as 2028 forecast at $1.69b. Simply Wall St applies discounts to each of these annual cash flows. As a result, the projected $2.11b in 2035 equates to a discounted figure of $872.20m in today’s terms.

Combining all discounted cash flows gives an estimated intrinsic value of US$131.33 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$145.89, the DCF output suggests the stock is about 11.1% overvalued based on these assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: Tapestry Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of earnings. It links the share price directly to the company’s profits, which is often what ultimately drives long term returns.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E.

Tapestry currently trades on a P/E of 56.6x. This sits well above the Luxury industry average P/E of 19.6x and also above the peer average of 24.9x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Tapestry is 28.6x, which is its own estimate of what P/E might be reasonable given factors like earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risks.

The Fair Ratio can be more useful than just comparing with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for Tapestry’s specific growth profile, risk characteristics and profitability. With a current P/E of 56.6x versus a Fair Ratio of 28.6x, the multiple based view suggests the stock is overvalued.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Norfolk Southern (NSC) Net Margin Expansion To 23.6% Tests Bullish Efficiency Narrative
data.description:
Norfolk Southern (NSC) has just posted its latest numbers, with Q4 2025 revenue of US$2,974 million and basic EPS of US$2.86, setting the stage for its Q1 2026 update against a trailing 12 month backdrop of US$12.8 billion in revenue and basic EPS of US$12.76. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from US$2,993 million in Q1 2025 to US$3,110 million in Q2 2025 and US$3,103 million in Q3 2025, while quarterly basic EPS shifted from US$3.31 to US$3.41 and then to US$3.17 across the same periods, outlining how earnings power is tracking into the new year. With trailing net margin at 23.6% and reported earnings growth of 9.6% over the last year, the latest results keep profitability in focus for anyone weighing the balance of risks and rewards.

See our full analysis for Norfolk Southern.

With the headline figures in place, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely held narratives around Norfolk Southern’s growth, profitability and risk profile, and where the numbers push back on those stories.

See what the community is saying about Norfolk Southern

Advertisement

Margins and EPS trends behind the 23.6% net margin

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Norfolk Southern earned US$2.9b of net income on US$12.2b of revenue, which works out to a 23.6% net margin compared with 21.6% a year earlier and Basic EPS of US$12.76.
  • Analysts' consensus view links this higher margin profile to the PSR 2.0 and cost reduction plans. However, the quarterly pattern, with Basic EPS moving from US$3.31 in Q1 2025 to US$2.86 in Q4 2025 on broadly similar quarterly revenue between US$2.97b and US$3.11b, shows that earnings per share can still move around even when sales are relatively stable.
    • Supporters of the bullish story highlight productivity and fuel efficiency efforts, but the variation in quarterly net income between US$643 million and US$767 million over 2025 underlines that efficiency gains have not removed profit swings altogether.
    • Bulls also point to industrial development and service improvements as future volume supports, while the current data shows earnings growth of 9.6% over the past year already coming more from margin strength than from top line expansion.
Analysts who focus on PSR 2.0 and margin gains may want to see how this fits alongside the full bullish case for Norfolk Southern's longer term earnings story, especially given the recent EPS volatility and 23.6% net margin performance. 🐂 Norfolk Southern Bull Case

9.6% earnings growth versus ~4.4% revenue forecasts

  • Over the last year, earnings grew 9.6% while revenue in the dataset is characterized as running at about 4.4% annual growth, and analysts in the provided data expect earnings to grow roughly 9.05% per year with revenue at about 4.4% per year, both slower than the US market growth rates cited.
  • Bears argue that slower top line growth and exposure to issues like lower export coal pricing and trade policy could pressure this earnings profile, and the numbers give them some support because the gap between earnings growth and revenue growth relies on margins remaining at around 23.6% or better while the forecasts already assume revenue growth that is not especially fast.
    • Critics highlight that factors such as US$35 million of storm restoration costs and limits on pricing power in certain intermodal markets could affect how sustainable the 9.6% trailing earnings growth rate is against a revenue trend of roughly half that pace.
    • What stands out for cautious investors is that analysts still project earnings growth of about 9.05% per year even though projected revenue growth and the external risks listed, including tariffs and lower coal prices, could make that target harder to reach if margins were to come under pressure.
Skeptical investors weighing that 9.6% trailing earnings growth against slower revenue forecasts and the headwinds cited may want a closer look at how the cautious narrative frames these risks for Norfolk Southern. 🐻 Norfolk Southern Bear Case

P/E of 25x versus industry and DCF fair value

  • Norfolk Southern trades on a trailing P/E of 25x, which is below the US Transportation industry average of 41.5x and the reported peer average of 44.4x, while the current share price of US$319.71 sits above the US$225.72 DCF fair value and above the US$317.67 analyst price target.
  • Consensus narrative notes that a 1.69% dividend yield and lower P/E than peers help the investment case. However, the fact that the shares are above both the DCF fair value of US$225.72 and the US$317.67 analyst target means valuation is not clear cut.
    • Supportive investors may focus on the combination of 9.6% earnings growth, 23.6% net margin and a P/E that is well below the 41.5x industry level as justification for the current price of US$319.71.
    • Others will point out that the high reported debt level and the gap between the current price and the DCF fair value, along with revenue growth forecasts around 4.4% per year, are all factors that could make the valuation look demanding despite the relative P/E discount.

Next Steps

With all that in mind, do these earnings and valuation trade offs leave you feeling cautious or optimistic, and how quickly do you want to firm up your own stance by weighing 4 key rewards and 1 important warning sign?

data.title: Waste Connections Uses AI To Shape Margins Retention And M&A Outlook
data.description:
• Waste Connections (NYSE:WCN) is rolling out AI-driven pricing tools that are materially affecting operating performance.
  • The company reports visible margin expansion linked to these AI tools, along with stronger pricing retention and better employee and customer retention.
  • Special waste volume growth and continued M&A activity are additional areas of focus alongside the AI initiatives.

Waste Connections, a major solid waste services provider, is using artificial intelligence to refine how it prices contracts and manages customer relationships. For you as an investor, the key point is that management links these tools directly to margin expansion and stickier customer relationships. That is in addition to existing efforts in special waste, which can carry different economics than traditional municipal routes.

The company highlights the cumulative effect of these AI programs as a factor supporting margin improvement over time. Together with an active M&A pipeline, the AI rollout is becoming a core part of how Waste Connections presents its operational and financial trajectory rather than a side project.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Waste Connections by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Waste Connections.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 4 things going right for Waste Connections that every investor should see.

For investors, the key takeaway is that Waste Connections is trying to turn AI from a buzzword into a set of measurable operating levers. Management links AI-powered pricing and retention tools to a 90 basis point underlying margin expansion in Q1 2026, a 20% improvement in customer retention and pricing effectiveness, and voluntary employee turnover below 10%. That sits alongside 6% solid waste core price and 8% growth in special waste tons, which together help explain why revenue in the quarter reached US$2,370.63m even as net income was US$219.34m compared with US$241.51m a year earlier. The AI rollout is also paired with an active M&A plan, with about US$100m of annualized revenue expected to close by early Q3 2026, and management describing 2026 as on track for an above average acquisition year.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Waste Connections Narrative

  • The focus on AI-enabled pricing, special waste growth, and acquisitions supports the existing narrative that pricing discipline, efficiency gains, and deal integration are key drivers of revenue and margin expansion.
  • Heavier use of acquisitions and technology spending could pressure margins if integration is slower than planned or if AI projects do not scale as anticipated, which would challenge assumptions that earnings growth and profitability continue to build cleanly from here.
  • The link between AI and improved employee retention, as well as specific targets for cumulative margin appreciation by 2028, adds detail that is not fully captured in the higher level discussion of operating efficiencies in the narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Waste Connections to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The company relies on a steady acquisition pipeline and carries a high level of debt, so any slowdown in M&A integration or change in financing conditions could weigh on returns.
  • ⚠️ AI initiatives span seven programs across hundreds of locations, which brings execution risk if tools are not adopted consistently or if benefits differ by region when compared with peers such as Waste Management, Republic Services, and GFL Environmental.
  • 🎁 Earnings grew strongly over the past year and analysts see further earnings growth, supported by price led solid waste revenue, special waste gains, and cost savings efforts.
  • 🎁 The shares are flagged as trading below some fair value estimates and analyst targets while still scoring positively on revenue and earnings growth, which some investors may view as a potential upside skew if execution on AI and M&A stays on track.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the focus is on whether AI-driven pricing and retention benefits keep showing up in reported margins and customer churn data. The Google Cloud Partner of the Year awards tie directly to EPAM’s executive agenda, because they validate how leadership is positioning the company around AI, sustainability, and complex cloud work rather than traditional staff augmentation. The recognition for sustainability solutions and an enterprise database migration for a European financial institution points to EPAM’s ability to win large, long-cycle projects against global players such as Accenture, Cognizant, and Infosys. For you as an investor, this matters less as a marketing headline and more as a real world check on whether management’s focus areas are resonating with big budget clients at a time when the share price has been under pressure. The awards also suggest that EPAM’s leadership team is executing on its push into higher value consulting and end to end transformation projects, which ties into prior commentary about returns on capital and earnings growth.   • The recognition for AI and cloud solutions lines up with the narrative that EPAM is moving up the value chain by focusing on complex, ROI driven transformation work rather than lower margin custom development.
  • These awards raise the bar for execution. If EPAM cannot keep winning similar large scale projects while competitors like Accenture and Tata Consultancy Services push hard in AI services, that would challenge the growth story.
  • The sustainability award, tied to geospatial and AI capabilities, introduces an environmental solutions angle that is not fully reflected in the broader narrative about AI driven transformation and margin expansion.

  • ⚠️ As hyperscalers like Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and AWS expand their own services arms, EPAM could face tighter competition for the kind of projects that underpinned these awards.

  • 🎁 The sustainability focused award highlights an additional use case for EPAM’s AI and data skills, potentially widening the opportunity set across regulated and ESG oriented clients.

From here, the useful indicators are not the awards themselves but the follow through. Watch for management commentary on how often these Google Cloud wins help EPAM get invited to larger requests for proposals, whether the company references new AI and sustainability engagements in earnings calls, and how concentrated these projects are by client or region. Approach 1: Rolls-Royce Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return.

For Rolls-Royce Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about £3.6b. Analysts provide cash flow estimates for several years ahead, and Simply Wall St then extends these out further. By 2030, the projected free cash flow used in the model is about £4.3b, with interim annual projections between 2026 and 2035 based on a mix of analyst sources and extrapolated estimates.

For a profitable company like Rolls-Royce Holdings, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is generating. A higher or lower P/E often reflects what investors expect for future growth and how much risk they see in those earnings. Strong growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower one.

  • Clean energy and next generation projects, including SMRs and UltraFan, are treated as potentially meaningful long term contributors, while the narrative also flags execution, regulation and capital intensity as important watchpoints.

🐻 Rolls-Royce Holdings Bear Case

Fair Value: £8.36

Implied overvaluation vs last close: around 26%.

Assumed revenue growth: 3.5% a year.

  • The bearish cohort instead works with assumptions of slower revenue growth and a sharp compression in margins over the next few years, which are used to derive expected earnings of about £1.7b by 2028.
  • Key concerns in this view include dependence on widebody engines, higher ongoing costs from supply chain and decarbonization pressures, and the drag from pension and debt obligations.
  • On this view, ambitious projects such as UltraFan and SMRs are seen as carrying enough timing and execution risk that a lower Fair Value is judged more appropriate than the current market price.

Taken together, these Narratives present a wide but transparent range for what Rolls-Royce Holdings could be worth. The range depends on how confident you are about growth in core aerospace and Power Systems, the pace of margin change, and the potential payoff from future technology and clean energy projects.

Recent returns and income profile

Canadian Apartment Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX:CAR.UN) has produced mixed returns across different periods, with a 0.4% gain over the past day, a 3.0% rise over the past month, and a 4.3% decline over the past 3 months.

Over longer periods, total returns show a 0.1% decline year to date, a 6.6% decline over the past year, a 17.4% decline over 3 years, and a 20.6% decline over 5 years, putting recent price moves into a broader context for unitholders.

At a last close of CA$37.07, the trust reports annual revenue of CA$1.00b and net income of CA$197.05m, entirely from residential real estate, with most revenue coming from Canada and a smaller contribution from the Netherlands and other parts of Europe.

Recent trading suggests momentum is softening, with a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 6.6% and a 3 year total shareholder return decline of 17.4%, despite a modest 1 month share price gain.

If you are comparing Canadian Apartment Properties REIT with other opportunities in real assets and infrastructure, this could be a good moment to scan the market using the 33 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks

With total returns under pressure over 1, 3 and 5 years, yet trading at an indicated discount to some valuation estimates, should you view Canadian Apartment Properties REIT as a value opportunity, or assume markets already price in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 17.4% Undervalued

With a last close of CA$37.07 against a narrative fair value of CA$44.90, the current unit price sits below where the most followed thesis places it, and that view is built on a detailed set of growth, margin and valuation assumptions.

The ongoing shift of CAPREIT's portfolio toward newer, well-located, low CapEx Canadian apartments with largely unregulated rents, combined with divestments of lower-performing and regulated assets in both Canada and Europe, positions the company to capture outsized rent growth and higher net operating income (NOI) in the context of a persistent housing undersupply and affordability crisis, directly benefiting revenue and margin expansion.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that confidence in higher margins and earnings per unit? The narrative leans on a specific blend of rent growth, cost control and unit buybacks that you may want to test against your own expectations.

According to this widely followed view, a discount rate of about 7.3% is applied to CAPREIT's future cash flows, with relatively modest revenue growth assumptions and much higher forecast profitability, to arrive at a fair value of CA$44.90 per unit.

The same thesis also hinges on analysts expecting earnings to rise meaningfully over the next few years, while the number of units on issue is forecast to decline as buybacks continue, which together support higher earnings per unit even if top line growth stays relatively restrained.

On this basis, CAPREIT is framed as undervalued relative to both the consensus price target of around CA$44.90 and the narrative fair value estimate, but the outcome still depends on whether future earnings and margins track close to those forecasts.

Result: Fair Value of CA$44.90 (UNDERVALUED)
